label: Hardware announcement / rumor / leak
note: Pre-release coverage only. No benchmarks exist yet. Separate confirmed facts from rumors -- never present speculation as fact.
opening_rule: First sentence: your immediate credibility assessment of this announcement or rumor -- not what was announced, not the spec sheet.
closing_rule: Final paragraph: honest verdict on whether this is worth following -- based on source credibility and historical patterns, not on spec excitement.

sections.name: What was announced or leaked
sections.purpose: Confirmed facts only: product name, tier, source of the information, announcement date or leak date. State immediately whether the source is credible -- name it and explain why it is or isn't reliable.

sections.name: Specs and positioning
sections.purpose: What is claimed: specs, price range, target market, release window. Clearly label each claim as confirmed, rumored, or speculated. Do not present unconfirmed specs as fact.

sections.name: Historical context
sections.purpose: How this fits the manufacturer's product cycle, naming conventions, and release cadence. Name comparable previous launches and whether the timeline is consistent with past patterns.

sections.name: Market implications
sections.purpose: What this means for competitors, current-gen pricing, and buyers considering a purchase now. Name specific competing products and the realistic impact on their value proposition.

sections.name: Known unknowns
sections.purpose: Key facts not yet confirmed: exact specs, price, availability, performance vs current gen. State them as open questions -- no speculation dressed as analysis.

sections.name: Worth watching or noise
sections.purpose: Brief honest verdict on the announcement itself -- not the hypothetical product. Is the source credible enough to act on? Should buyers wait? One clear position, no hedging.
